unverified.label: Bootstrapping Real-World RL
unverified.quote: I think in practice the realistic scenario is one where it starts with supervised learning, behavioral cloning; humans do the work... And then over time, as it gets pretty good, you're starting fusing some reinforcement learning

unverified.label: Domain Randomization
unverified.quote: Instead of trying to somehow find a way to perfectly match it up; we say the things we don't know, we're going to vary them... if a single neural network can learn to control the robot, across all those simulators... it makes it actually very likely, it'll also succeed in the real world.

unverified.label: Hindsight Experience Replay
unverified.quote: No matter what the robot does, we're going to let it learn from it. We're going to say, okay, I didn't ask you to, let's say, fall down... But if I had asked you to fall down, you did the right thing.

unverified.label: Bootstrapped Reinforcement Learning Deployment
unverified.quote: I think in practice the realistic scenario is one where it starts with supervised learning, behavioral cloning; humans do the work... And then over time, as it gets pretty good, you're starting fusing some reinforcement learning where you give it actual objectives

unverified.label: The Research vs. Real-World Gap
unverified.quote: Research: 0 to 1. If achieving 70% on a benchmark, move on to the next one. Real-World: Even 90% is not enough! Exacerbated by long tail of scenarios

unverified.label: Software 2.0 Paradigm
unverified.quote: Traditional Programming: program by writing lines of code. Deep Learning (“Software 2.0”): program by providing data.

unverified.label: Ignoring the Perception-Action Loop
unverified.quote: Key Assumption: Decision 1 does NOT affect Request 2, … But: Reality of Perception-Action Loop

all_results.label: Robotics as the Ultimate Reality Check
all_results.quote: In the real world, the natural intelligence is all in physical embodiment. It seemed to me the most natural place to start to try to build AI is tied into physical systems; tied into robots, is a more natural way to make progress.

all_results.label: Software 2.0: Data Over Hard-Coded Rules
all_results.quote: Once you start putting in rules, then you have to put, another 'if then else', another 'if then else', and at some point, things become unwieldy.

all_results.label: AI as a Meta-Discipline
all_results.quote: Maybe by working on artificial intelligence, in some way, I could be working on everything, because maybe AI could help everything else.

all_results.label: Deep Learning as the Path to AGI
all_results.quote: In the last 10 years, every single breakthrough that has been of high impact in AI has been deep learning. There's literally been no exception that I'm aware of.

all_results.label: Bootstrapping Real-World RL
all_results.quote: I think in practice the realistic scenario is one where it starts with supervised learning, behavioral cloning; humans do the work... And then over time, as it gets pretty good, you're starting fusing some reinforcement learning

all_results.label: Sim2Real via Domain Randomization
all_results.quote: If the model sees enough simulated variation, the real world may look like just the next simulator

all_results.label: Human Preferences for Reward Design
all_results.quote: Putting (non-expert) humans into the agent learning loop!

all_results.label: Probabilistic Reasoning & Optimization as Foundations
all_results.quote: The majority of these techniques are heavily based on probabilistic reasoning and optimization---two areas with wide applicability in modern Artificial Intelligence.

all_results.label: Adaptive Policies over Perfect Models
all_results.quote: Learn an ***adaptive policy*** that can quickly adapt to any of the learned models

all_results.label: Hardware Designed for AI
all_results.quote: This robot is designed for the assumption that in the future, robots will be controlled much more intelligently by AI systems that use visual feedback, that use force feedback, much like how humans control their own arms.

all_results.label: Domain Randomization
all_results.quote: Instead of trying to somehow find a way to perfectly match it up; we say the things we don't know, we're going to vary them... if a single neural network can learn to control the robot, across all those simulators... it makes it actually very likely, it'll also succeed in the real world.

all_results.label: Hindsight Experience Replay
all_results.quote: No matter what the robot does, we're going to let it learn from it. We're going to say, okay, I didn't ask you to, let's say, fall down... But if I had asked you to fall down, you did the right thing.

all_results.label: Bootstrapped Reinforcement Learning Deployment
all_results.quote: I think in practice the realistic scenario is one where it starts with supervised learning, behavioral cloning; humans do the work... And then over time, as it gets pretty good, you're starting fusing some reinforcement learning where you give it actual objectives

all_results.label: Learning Reward from Preferences
all_results.quote: Step 1. Collect samples via interactions with environment Step 2. Collect human preferences Step 3. Optimize a reward model using cross entropy loss Step 4. Optimize a policy using off-policy algorithms

all_results.label: Model-Based RL via Meta Policy Optimization (MB-MPO)
all_results.quote: for iter = 1, 2, … collect data under current adaptive policies, learn ENSEMBLE of K simulators from all past data, meta-policy optimization over ENSEMBLE

all_results.label: Unsupervised RL as 'Play'
all_results.quote: If you transpose this onto humans, think of it as play. When you have children, they just play. They explore the world by play.

all_results.label: The Research vs. Real-World Gap
all_results.quote: Research: 0 to 1. If achieving 70% on a benchmark, move on to the next one. Real-World: Even 90% is not enough! Exacerbated by long tail of scenarios

all_results.label: Model-Bias (Overfitting in Model-based RL)
all_results.quote: policy optimization tends to exploit regions where insufficient data is available to train the model, leading to catastrophic failures

all_results.label: Randomize the Simulator
all_results.quote: If the model sees enough simulated variation, the real world may look like just the next simulator

all_results.label: Robotics as a Reality Check
all_results.quote: It's very easy to think you make a lot of progress, and then you try to see if a real robot can do something in a real environment that you quickly realize that the results weren’t great

all_results.label: Deep Networks for Patterns
all_results.quote: If there is a pattern we can probably represent it with a deep network and capture that pattern.

all_results.label: Physical Embodiment
all_results.quote: In the real world, the natural intelligence is all in physical embodiment. It seemed to me the most natural place to start to try to build AI is tied into physical systems; tied into robots.

all_results.quote: If the model sees enough simulated variation, the real world may look like just the next simulator

all_results.label: AI as Everything
all_results.quote: Maybe by working on artificial intelligence, in some way, I could be working on everything, because maybe AI could help everything else.

all_results.label: Software 2.0 Paradigm
all_results.quote: Traditional Programming: program by writing lines of code. Deep Learning (“Software 2.0”): program by providing data.

all_results.label: The Joy and Danger of RL
all_results.quote: Very few things more fun to watch than a reinforcement learning robot starting from nothing and inventing things. But it's just time consuming and it's not always safe.

all_results.label: Deep Learning Dominance
all_results.quote: In the last 10 years, every single breakthrough that has been of high impact in AI has been deep learning. There's literally been no exception that I'm aware of.

all_results.label: Mathematical Foundations
all_results.quote: The majority of these techniques are heavily based on probabilistic reasoning and optimization---two areas with wide applicability in modern Artificial Intelligence.

all_results.label: Hard-Coding Rules and Assumptions
all_results.quote: Once you start putting in rules, then you have to put, another 'if then else', another 'if then else', and at some point, things become unwieldy.

all_results.label: Deploying Pure RL from Scratch
all_results.quote: Very few things more fun to watch than a reinforcement learning robot starting from nothing and inventing things. But it's just time consuming and it's not always safe.

all_results.label: Evaluating AI Solely by Math Complexity
all_results.quote: ...to really try to see the connection from what you're working on to what impact they can really have, what change it can make rather than what's the math that happened to be in your work.
